label: Australian IO model metadata and concordance

regional_employment.publication: Census of Population and Housing 2021, Table G54
regional_employment.variable: Place of Work employment by ANZSIC division by SA4
regional_employment.note: Census data is point-in-time (August 2021). Next Census: 2026. SA4s with <1000 employed persons excluded.

regionalisation.delta_source: Lenzen, M. et al. (2017). Economic Systems Research 29(2): 275-295. Calibrated for Australia.
regionalisation.delta_note: Optimal range 0.2-0.3 per Flegg & Tohmo (2013). 0.3 used by Lenzen for Australian MRIO.
regionalisation.formula: lambda = [log2(1 + RE/NE)]^delta; FLQ(i,j) = CILQ(i,j) * lambda for i!=j, SLQ(i) for i==j; A_reg(i,j) = A_nat(i,j) * min(FLQ(i,j), 1)

type_1.label: Type I (direct + indirect)
type_1.description: Captures the initial expenditure plus supply chain purchases from local firms. Derived from column sums of the Leontief inverse L = (I - A_reg)^{-1}.

type_2.label: Type II (direct + indirect + induced)
type_2.description: Adds household consumption effects by augmenting the matrix with a household row/column. Assumes compensation of employees = 35% of output and marginal propensity to consume = 0.6.
type_2.note: Type II multipliers are higher and less certain. Use Type I as default.

anzsic_to_sic_concordance.description: Approximate mapping from ANZSIC divisions to UK SIC sections for cross-country comparison

sa4_note: SA4 (Statistical Area Level 4) is the ABS labour market region geography. 88 proper SA4s after filtering out 'no usual address', 'migratory', and 'other territories' categories.
limitations: IO models assume fixed input proportions (no substitution or efficiency gains)
No price effects (large investments can push up local costs)
Regional coefficients estimated from employment-based location quotients, not survey data
Census employment data is 2021 vintage; industry structure may have shifted
SA4 boundaries are statistical, not economic; impacts spill across boundaries
Cross-hauling (simultaneous import and export of the same commodity) is not captured
The ABS stopped publishing IO multipliers after 1998-99 specifically because of misuse concerns
Type II multipliers endogenise consumption, further inflating impacts
Results are indicative upper bounds, not predictions
